title: PLD and Komeito agree to normalize Parliament and postpone seat reduction to prioritize Imperial Household Law reform
summary: The ruling Liberal Democratic Party and its ally Komeito reach an agreement allowing the opposition's return to the plenary and postponing the seat debate in favor of imperial reform.

title: US bombs Iranian positions in retaliation for attacks on ships and Iran attacks US bases in Bahrain and Kuwait
summary: The military escalation between the United States and Iran intensifies with mutual attacks, while Japan opposes transit fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Payment company Zenshin declares bankruptcy with debts exceeding 100 billion yen
summary: Credit card processing company Zenshin collapses, leaving thousands of businesses affected by unpaid sales.

title: Japanese 10-year bond reaches 2.87%, highest level in 30 years, due to inflation and debt issuance fears
summary: Rising oil prices and uncertainty over bond issuance push Japanese sovereign debt yields to three-decade highs.

title: Argentina comes back against Egypt with Messi record, Switzerland advances on penalties, Spain eliminates Portugal, and Belgium crushes USA in the World Cup
summary: The football World Cup completes its quarterfinals with exciting matches: Argentina beats Egypt 3-2, Switzerland eliminates Colombia, Spain surpasses Portugal, and Belgium routs the United States.

title: Nordic combined is excluded from the Olympic program, sparking anger from Japanese medalist Watabe Akito
summary: The International Olympic Committee decides to remove Nordic combined from the Games, generating strong criticism in Japan for losing a medal opportunity.

title: Yukihiko Inoue, former Metropolitan Police chief who led the investigation into the Aum sarin gas attack, dies
summary: Inoue, who led the investigation that led to the capture of those responsible for the 1995 Tokyo subway attack, died at age 88.

title: Metropolitan Police sergeant arrested for leaking investigation information to illegal betting establishment
summary: The Metropolitan Police officer is arrested for allegedly providing case data to an illegal betting establishment.

title: Former GungHo executive arrested for embezzlement through false orders
summary: A former section chief at video game company GungHo is arrested for causing damages of about 40 million yen.

title: Asahi Group suffers cyberattack, reducing profits by 36.7% and causing million-dollar losses
summary: The cyberattack against the Japanese food and beverage giant forces it to revise its financial forecasts for fiscal 2025.

title: Shohei Ohtani hits his 300th home run in Major League Baseball, the fastest for a Japanese player in nine seasons
summary: The Angels batter becomes the first Japanese to reach 300 home runs in MLB, being the fifth fastest in history.

title: UN warns that only 15% of Sustainable Development Goals are on track to be achieved
summary: The UN progress report reveals that advances have stalled or reversed due to conflicts and economic crises.

title: Children's author and illustrator Akiko Hayashi dies at 81
summary: Author of classic books like 'The First Errand' and 'Kon to Aki,' Hayashi is remembered for her ability to capture children's emotions.

title: Rainy season ends in northern Kyushu, Chugoku, and Kinki, with high temperatures from morning
summary: The end of the tsuyu brings intense heat to wide areas of Japan, expected to continue in the coming days.

title: Five telephone companies delay introduction of prefix 060 due to display problems on some phones
summary: The operators postpone the start of the new area code because certain terminals cannot display numbers correctly.

title: Fair Trade Commission sanctions Noritsu for storing thousands of subcontractors' molds without cost
summary: The water heater manufacturer is reprimanded for violating subcontracting law by demanding free storage of 5,000 molds.

title: Senate constitutional committee agrees to vote on 15th on referendum law reform
summary: The commission in charge of constitutional reform reaches a consensus to put the changes to the referendum law to a vote.

title: Nikkei 225 index falls for third consecutive day, closes at lowest level in a month
summary: The Tokyo stock market suffers losses due to tensions in the Middle East and the decline of South Korean tech stocks.

title: Prosecutor who said 'don't underestimate the prosecution' faces unprecedented criminal trial as debate on reform of the Public Prosecutor's Office grows
summary: The case of a prosecutor accused of abuse of power reignites discussion on the need to reform the Japanese prosecution office.

title: Linear Chuo Shinkansen maglev train would double its estimated cost to 11 trillion yen, although it would shorten the trip to Osaka to one hour
summary: The maglev train project that will connect Tokyo and Nagoya faces cost overruns and delays, but promises to dramatically reduce travel times once completed.
